<div class="blog-header">

# Kelly Criterion: Avoid Ruin

<div class="blog-subtitle">

Don't ruin your reputation or get wiped to zero

</div>

<div class="podcast-container style-1">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

<div class="date">

Jul 10 2019

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

**Nivi:**<span style=""> Let’s chat about the Kelly criterion. </span>

**Naval:**<span style=""> The Kelly criterion is a popularized
mathematical formulation of a simple concept. The simple concept is:
Don’t risk everything. Stay out of jail. Don’t bet everything on one big
gamble. Be careful how much you bet each time, so you don’t lose the
whole kitty. </span>

<span style="">If you’re a gambler, the Kelly criterion mathematically
formulates how much you should wager per hand</span><span style="">,
even if you have an edge—</span><span style="">because even when you
have an edge, you can still lose. Let’s say you have 51-to-49 edge.
Every gambler knows not to bet the whole kitty on that 51-to-49
edge</span><span style="">—</span><span style="">because you could lose
everything and won’t get to come back to the average.</span>

<span id="more-28493258"></span>

<span style="">Nassim Taleb famously talks about
</span>[<span style="">ergodicity</span>](https://medium.com/incerto/the-logic-of-risk-taking-107bf41029d3)<span style="">,
which is a fancy word for a simple concept: What is true for 100 people
on average isn’t the same as one person averaging that same thing 100
times. </span>

**Ruining your reputation is the same as getting wiped to zero**

<span style="">The easiest way to see that is with
</span>[<span style="">Russian
roulette</span>](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russian_roulette)<span style="">.
Say six people play Russian roulette one time each, and each winner gets
\$1 billion. One person ends up dead and five people get \$1 billion.
Compare that to one person playing Russian roulette six times with the
same gun. They are never going to end up a billionaire—they will be dead
and worth zero. So risk-taking—especially when the averages that are
calculated across large populations—is not always rational. </span>

<span style="">The Kelly criterion helps you avoid ruin. The number one
way people get ruined in modern business is not by betting too much;
it’s by cutting corners and doing unethical or downright illegal things.
Ending up in an orange jumpsuit in prison or ruining your reputation is
the same as getting wiped to zero—so never do those things.</span>

</div>

</div>
